project_metadata.name: High-Precision Scientific Computing & Deep Learning Workspace
project_metadata.description: A specialized environment for high-precision numerical simulations and deep learning implementations from scratch using C, Go, and Python.

topics.subject: DNN Implementation from Scratch
topics.description: Implementation of Deep Neural Networks without external libraries. Focuses on manual weight management, backpropagation, and activation functions like Sigmoid, Tanh, and ReLU.

topics.subject: Parallel Computing with Goroutines
topics.description: Utilizing Go's concurrency primitives (Goroutines and WaitGroups) to parallelize neuron calculations and weight updates in DNNs, significantly improving training performance.

knowledge_base.category: Scientific Simulations (数値シミュレーション)

topics.subject: High-Precision Numerical Analysis
topics.description: Using 'long double' in C for maximum precision in physics and financial modeling. Key models include Lorenz systems and magnetic field calculations.

topics.subject: Break-Even Point (BEP) Analysis
topics.description: Mathematical modeling of profitability. Calculations include contribution margin ratio, break-even sales, and safety margin. Recently integrated with DNN for predictive modeling.

guidelines: Use 'long double' and corresponding math functions (sqrtl, tanhl, powl) for scientific calculations.
Prefer explicit memory management for neural network layers in C.
Serialize weights to .bin files and results to .csv for analysis.
All compiled binaries should be stored in the bin/ directory.

file_mappings.src/c: Core C source code for high-precision simulations and DNN libraries.
file_mappings.src/go: Go implementations focusing on parallel execution and performance.
file_mappings.mathp: Mathematical logic, BEP analysis, and experimental scripts (Python/Go/C).
